title: HMO, PPO, EPO, and HSA
description: Practice understanding of HMOs, PPOs, EPOs, and HSAs with HDHP

assessment_items.question_content:
Certain health insurance plans require you to see *only* [[☃ definition 1]] doctors.

**Which plan has this requirement?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation:
- HMO : Health Maintenance Organization  
- PPO : Preferred Provider Organization  
- EPO : Exclusive Provider Organization  
- HSA : Health Savings Account 
- HDHP : High-Deductible Health Plan
options.showPrompt: What do these acronyms stand for?

assessment_items.question_content:
Certain health insurance plans allow you to see *both* [[☃ definition 1]] and [[☃ definition 2]] doctors.

**Which plan allows this to happen?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.definition: does not have a contract with your health insurance plan

options.explanation:
- HMO : Health Maintenance Organization  
- PPO : Preferred Provider Organization  
- EPO : Exclusive Provider Organization  
- HSA : Health Savings Account 
- HDHP : High-Deductible Health Plan
options.showPrompt: What do these acronyms stand for?

assessment_items.question_content:
Certain health insurance plans allow you to see a specialist without a referral from an [[☃ definition 1]] doctor.

**Which plan allows you to do this?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation:
- HMO : Health Maintenance Organization  
- PPO : Preferred Provider Organization  
- EPO : Exclusive Provider Organization  
- HSA : Health Savings Account 
- HDHP : High-Deductible Health Plan
options.showPrompt: What do these acronyms stand for?

assessment_items.question_content:
Many people choose [[☃ definition 1]] plans for a specific, important reason.

**What is one of the main advantages of selecting an HMO?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: can see any doctor, including outside of network
no referrals needed to see a specialist

assessment_items.question_content:
Many people choose [[☃ definition 1]] plans for a specific, important reason.

**What is a main benefit of a PPO?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: freedom to see any doctor, even one outside the network
one main doctor who handles all of your healthcare.

assessment_items.question_content:
Many people choose [[☃ definition 1]]s over other health plans. 

**What is one benefit of a HDHP?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Health insurance plans cost different amounts each month because they offer different benefits.

**Which plan is typically the least expensive?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation:
- HMO : Health Maintenance Organization  
- PPO : Preferred Provider Organization  
- EPO : Exclusive Provider Organization  
- HSA : Health Savings Account 
- HDHP : High-Deductible Health Plan
options.showPrompt: What do these acronyms stand for?

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation:
- HMO : Health Maintenance Organization

assessment_items.question_content:
Health insurance plans cost different amounts each month because they offer different benefits.

**Which plan usually falls into the middle for cost?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation:
- HMO : Health Maintenance Organization  
- PPO : Preferred Provider Organization  
- EPO : Exclusive Provider Organization  
- HSA : Health Savings Account 
- HDHP : High-Deductible Health Plan
options.showPrompt: What do these acronyms stand for?

assessment_items.question_content:
Your employer provides an attractive benefit: every month they will contribute $\$200$ to a Health Savings Account (HSA) for you. This contribution can help pay for your medical expenses.

**Which plan do you need to pick to get this free HSA money?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation:
- HMO : Health Maintenance Organization  
- PPO : Preferred Provider Organization  
- EPO : Exclusive Provider Organization  
- HSA : Health Savings Account 
- HDHP : High-Deductible Health Plan
options.showPrompt: What do these acronyms stand for?

assessment_items.question_content:
You picked a High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP) because it has lower monthly costs. You also found it has an extra benefit you didn’t expect.

**What is this extra benefit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: access to any specialist without a referral
a savings account for medical expenses

assessment_items.question_content:
You want a health insurance option that lets you save the most money, offers the best tax benefits, and comes with some of the lowest premiums. Among the available choices, one combination stands out in providing these benefits.

**Which option should you choose?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation:
- HMO : Health Maintenance Organization  
- PPO : Preferred Provider Organization  
- EPO : Exclusive Provider Organization  
- HSA : Health Savings Account 
- HDHP : High-Deductible Health Plan
options.showPrompt: What do these acronyms stand for?
